question.text: When annuity/interest rates fall during retirement, what is the recommended approach?

answer.text: Invest all funds in a single annuity immediately at retirement
Stagger investments across different time periods to average out rate fluctuations
Move everything to equity to chase higher returns
Withdraw the entire corpus and keep it in a savings account

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Staggering investments across different time periods helps average out interest rate fluctuations. This is similar to the rupee cost averaging concept applied to the distribution phase. **The Trap:** Investing everything in a single annuity at one point in time exposes the retiree to the risk of locking in at a low rate. **Cross-Exam Context:** This strategy is analogous to a bond ladder or CD ladder approach used in Western financial planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise retirees to invest in fixed income instruments with staggered maturities (1-year, 3-year, 5-year FDs) rather than putting everything in one long-term deposit.
